points.content: A longitudinal study found that loneliness is more closely linked to lapses in immediate and delayed recall than to the overall speed of cognitive decline.

points.title: Vampire Survivors’ new spinoff switches genres but keeps the good vibes
points.content: When Vampire Survivors first exploded onto the scene, it was pretty much all I could think about. The formula of jumping into runs, taking on thousands of enemies, and becoming absurdly overpowered kept me picking up the game again and again - Steam says I've played it for more than 60 hours. Over time, though, […]

points.title: This pasta sauce wants to record your family
points.content: As if there weren't already enough devices listening in on everything being said in your home, Prego, the pasta and pizza sauce brand, is releasing a device designed to record everything said around the dinner table for posterity. The Connection Keeper, which looks like an oversized pasta jar lid, was created in collaboration with StoryCorps, […]

points.title: Huawei beats Samsung and Apple to market with the first wide foldable
points.content: Huawei has launched its passport-style foldable in China, ahead of similar devices said to be coming from Apple and Samsung. That makes the Pura X Max the first wide foldable phone that's actually available to buy, with today's launch now giving us the full specifications and price after its design was unveiled by Huawei last […]

points.title: DJI’s latest power station is proof that good things come in mini packages
points.content: As an avid vanlifer, I have to carefully curate everything I carry in order to eke out every square inch of storage I can to support life off the grid for weeks at a time. That's why I jumped at the chance to review DJI's latest power station. The DJI Power 1000 Mini is almost […]

points.title: GoPro’s new Mission cameras are priced beyond most weekend athletes
points.content: GoPro is pricing its new Mission 1 and Mission 1 Pro cameras at $599.99 and $699.99, respectively, each discounted by $100 if you subscribe to its ecosystem - fine for professional filmmakers, but a tough sell for weekend warriors. The Mission 1 and Mission 1 Pro can both be reserved now, and you'll get the […]

points.title: We might finally know how to use quantum computers to boost AI
points.content: Pushing against years of scepticism, an analysis suggests quantum computers may offer real advantages for running machine learning and similar algorithms in the near future

points.title: US Homebuilders Set for Another ‘Lost’ Earnings Season
points.content: For US homebuilders, the Iran war dashed what little optimism they had left for this earnings season.

points.title: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazilian Miner in $2.8 Billion Deal
points.content: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a $2.8 billion cash-and-stock transaction, marking one of the largest deals in the rare-earths industry.

points.title: Iran Wavers on Peace Talks & UAE Explores US Financial Lifeline: WSJ | The Pulse 04/20/2026
points.content: "The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops. Today's guests: Dominic Bunning, Nomura, Head of G-10 FX Strategy; Jumana Saleheen, Vanguard, Chief European Economist; Maria Simeonova, European Council on Foreign Relations, Head of Sofia Office; Hasan Alhasan, International Institute for Strategic Studies, Middle East Policy Senior Fellow; Jill Rutter, Institute for Government, Senior Fellow (Source: Bloomberg)

points.title: Psychedelic Stocks Soar as Trump Signs Order to Ease Access
points.content: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-related therapies are surging in premarket trading Monday after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access.

points.title: Strategy Buys $2.54 Billion of Bitcoin, Most Since November 2024
points.content: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $2.54 billion in Bitcoin over the previous seven days, marking the digital asset treasury company’s largest acquisition of the original cryptocurrency since November 2024.

points.title: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure on Oil Shipments on Hormuz Halt
points.content: Kuwait declared force majeure on shipments of crude oil and refined products as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz makes it impossible to meet obligations to customers that can’t bring vessels into the Persian Gulf.

points.title: Eni and Repsol Plan to Start Venezuela Gas Exports in 2031
points.content: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.

points.title: Congo’s Debut Eurobond to Help Diversify Economy, Minister Says
points.content: The Democratic Republic of Congo will use the proceeds of its debut bond sale to invest in hydropower and transport infrastructure, part of an effort to diversify the resource-rich Central African nation, Finance Minister Doudou Fwamba Likunde said.

points.title: Iran Hesitant on Talks as Tensions Rise After US Seizes Ship
points.content: Iran expressed reluctance to send diplomats to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks after the US maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and seized an Iranian ship.

points.title: Blackstone-Backed Jersey Mike’s Submits Confidential IPO Filing
points.content: Jersey Mike’s Subs, the sandwich chain with more than 3,000 locations, submitted a confidential filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission for its initial public offering.

points.title: Brazil Economists Lift Selic 2026, 2027 Forecasts on Oil Spike
points.content: Brazil economists raised their forecasts for the benchmark interest rate for this year and next as an energy price spike from the war in Iran reverberates though Latin America’s biggest economy.

points.title: BofA’s Blanch Warns Jet Fuel, Oil Flows Remian at Risk After Conflict
points.content: Francisco Blanch, head of commodities and derivatives research at BofA Securities, discusses the post-conflict issues facing the global oil market, the availability of jet fuel, and the potential timeframe for restoring oil flows once the war in Iran comes to an end. (Source: Bloomberg)

points.title: US Stocks Halt Five-Day Rally as Ceasefire Extension In Doubt
points.content: US stocks slipped on Monday as President Donald Trump said an extension to the ceasefire with Iran was unlikely.

points.title: US Premarket Movers for April 20, 2026
points.content: S P 500 Index futures fall 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after the US maintained a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and seized an Iranian ship.

points.title: India’s RBI Eases Some Curbs on Banks’ Forex Market Trades
points.content: India’s central bank eased some restrictions on lenders’ currency market transactions, signaling a partial rollback in some of its most aggressive steps in decades to defend the rupee.

points.title: Wall Street Strategists Bullish on the US Earnings Outlook
points.content: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are upbeat on the outlook for US earnings after a positive start to the first-quarter reporting season.

points.title: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
points.content: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

points.title: Chaos in Hormuz Should Be a Reality Check for Oil Prices
points.content: Commodity flows from the Persian Gulf are back at a near-standstill, a reminder of the intractable nature of the war in Iran.

points.title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises After US Seizes Iran-Flagged Ship | Bloomberg Brief 4/20/2026
points.content: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US Navy attacked and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. Iran says it has no plans to attend potential negotiations with the US in Pakistan with the ceasefire expiring on Tuesday. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses the market volatility amid renewed uncertainties around the war. (Source: Bloomberg)

points.title: Energy Services Set to Benefit Once Iran War Ends, Barclays Says
points.content: The European energy services sector faces a brighter future once the Middle East conflict de-escalates as a result of work that oil producers need to undertake to resolve disruption, according to Barclays Plc analysts.

points.title: Markets Still Have a Lot of Hope and Optimism, Says NewEdge’s Dawson
points.content: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, explains what there is to still like about markets and sees earnings growth as idiosyncratic and not broad based. (Source: Bloomberg)

points.title: UnitedHealth Isn’t Out of the Woods With $6 Billion Medicare Hit
points.content: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s results Tuesday will reflect the full effect of Medicare payment changes set in motion three years ago that cost the company billions, cratering profits last year and blindsiding investors.

points.title: German Banks Aren’t Panicking Over Mythos AI Threat, Sewing Says
points.content: Germany’s banks are well-prepared for heightened cyber risks as Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence model sparks global fears of a new era for computer hacks, said Deutsche Bank AG Chief Executive Officer Christian Sewing.

points.title: Fitch Cuts Philippines’ Rating Outlook as Growth Prospects Dim
points.content: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying a decline in public investment and rising energy costs have created risks to the Southeast Asian nation’s economic growth.

points.title: The Iran crisis has not yet peaked
points.content: The war is currently more likely to escalate than to be resolved by negotiation

points.title: Japan downgrades tsunami warning after strong earthquake
points.content: No reports of major damage from waves that hit north-eastern coastline after magnitude 7.4 tremor

points.title: Reform UK says it will deport at least 200,000 refugees
